task_completion.description: The agent opened NASA Panoply and loaded the required NetCDF datasets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application window is open, and the datasets 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' and 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' are visible in the sources/datasets panel.

task_completion.description: The agent created a geographic map plot for Sea Surface Temperature for the month of September.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is visible displaying a map of Sea Surface Temperature (SST). The time/date setting in the plot window must be set to September (or time index 9).

task_completion.description: The agent created a geographic map plot for Precipitation Rate for the month of September.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is visible displaying a map of Precipitation Rate. The time/date setting in the plot window must be set to September (or time index 9).

task_completion.description: The agent exported the generated maps as PNG images to the RecoveryOps directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window, terminal, or Panoply save dialog showing 'sst_september.png' and 'precip_september.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/RecoveryOps/' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent created the structured recommendation report in the RecoveryOps directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the creation or editing of a file named 'splashdown_recommendation.txt' in the '~/Documents/RecoveryOps/' directory.

task_completion.description: The recommendation report identifies Bravo as the safer site and includes the correct SST values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text of the report is visible and explicitly recommends Site Bravo. It should state that Alpha's SST is approximately 28.6°C (or between 28-31°C) and Bravo's SST is approximately 20.2°C (or between 19-24°C).

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply graphical interface to generate the plots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Panoply GUI (menus, plot windows, array views) rather than using a Python script or CLI tool to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the SST values by interacting with the data in Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent using Panoply's array view, plot cursor, or tooltip to read the data values at the specific coordinates for Alpha and Bravo, rather than hallucinating the values.

integrity.description: The agent manually drafted the recommendation report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal echo/cat commands should be visible showing the agent typing or constructing the report, rather than running a pre-existing script to generate it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Site Alpha (Gulf of Mexico) has a September SST of approximately 28.6°C (acceptable range: 28.0-31.0°C), which exceeds the 26.5°C tropical cyclone threshold. Site Bravo (off Baja California) has a September SST of approximately 20.2°C (acceptable range: 19.0-24.0°C), which is safely below the threshold. The correct recommendation is Site Bravo. Expected output files are sst_september.png, precip_september.png, and splashdown_recommendation.txt located in ~/Documents/RecoveryOps/.
